mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, a deceleration in the rate of monthly job losses (a positive second derivative in payrolls) signals the labor market has bottomed. This improves forward-looking sentiment and drives equity appreciation over the medium term as recovery expectations are priced in.
candidate_trigger: The 3-month change of the 1-month change in PAYEMS is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:US economy is in a deep recession as of May 2009, making bottoming signals highly relevant.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following the troughs of the 1991, 2001, and 2008 recessions.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, highly accommodative monetary policy forces investors out of safe assets and into riskier assets like equities in search of yield. This creates a persistent positive tailwind for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return during periods when the trigger is active is not statistically significantly greater than the long-term average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The 3-month rate of change in industrial production serves as a real-time proxy for corporate earnings momentum. Positive momentum indicates improving business conditions and drives positive short-term equity returns, while negative momentum signals contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a widely followed coincident economic indicator.
failure_condition: The rolling 36-month rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and contracting industrial production signals a stagflationary environment. This simultaneously erodes corporate profit margins via input costs and increases equity discount rates, leading to strongly negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change > 4% AND year-over-year INDPRO change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a prolonged bear market for US equities.
failure_condition: In historical periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative month-over-month surprise in retail sales indicates an abrupt contraction in consumer demand. This negatively impacts expected corporate revenues for consumer-facing sectors, leading to an immediate negative market reaction.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes approximately 2/3 of US GDP.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low levels of housing starts signify a deep crisis in the housing sector. This has negative wealth effects for consumers and creates systemic risk in the financial system, depressing equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2008 financial crisis was precipitated by a collapse in the US housing market.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits a positive average 60-day return during historical periods when HOUST is below 750 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rapidly rising unemployment rate signals deep economic distress, reducing consumer confidence and aggregate demand. This lowers corporate earnings expectations across the board and drives negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment is a key lagging indicator used by the NBER to date recessions.
failure_condition: The signal has a hit rate below 55% for predicting negative 20-day returns during NBER-dated recessions since 1970.

mechanism_narrative: When real economic growth is weak, an acceleration in inflation is particularly damaging. It signals the Federal Reserve has limited capacity to provide monetary stimulus, leading to a contraction in equity valuation multiples.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in GDPC1 < 1.5% AND Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:This 'growth scare' dynamic limits policy response options.
failure_condition: In historical periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day S&P 500 forward return is positive.
